text: Code reviews are an essential practice in software development. When developers check each other’s code, they often catch bugs early, before the code reaches production. This saves time and money by preventing bigger problems later. Another major benefit is knowledge sharing. Team members learn new techniques and better understand different parts of the codebase. This reduces the risk of one person being the only expert in a specific area. Over time, regular code reviews improve team quality by encouraging consistency and higher standards. Ultimately, they create a culture of collaboration and help everyone grow as programmers.
